text.title: VUI Conversation Design Antipatterns
text.summary: Common VUI design failures and agent-specific failure modes to prevent.

antipattern.description: Disambiguation with more than one clarifying turn before falling back to directed prompt or human.
antipattern.reason: Multi-turn disambiguation compounds confusion. After one failed disambiguation, transition to a directed prompt with explicit options or escalate to human assistance.

antipattern.description: Using option prompts with more than 4 choices (e.g., listing 7 options).
antipattern.reason: Voice working memory holds 3-4 items. Users lose track of options mid-list and cannot re-hear. Constrain options to 4 or fewer, or use directed prompting to narrow the space.

antipattern.description: Breaking the user's mental model on barge-in by ignoring a relevant interruption mid-prompt.
antipattern.reason: If the user starts speaking during a prompt with a relevant utterance and the system ignores it, trust breaks immediately. Accept relevant barge-in; if irrelevant, gracefully resume.

antipattern.description: LLM-generated unbounded paraphrases used as runtime prompt variants.
antipattern.reason: Runtime LLM paraphrases drift in tone and length across sessions. Pre-generate 3-5 vetted variants offline and rotate. Never generate prompt copy live in production.

antipattern.description: LLM dialog flow skips no-input and max-retry transitions.
antipattern.reason: LLMs reliably omit timeout and escalation transitions. Run repair-coverage-checker on every draft before review — treat missing transitions as blocking defects.

antipattern.description: Using the same TTS engine for test fixtures as for product output.
antipattern.reason: Same-vendor TTS inflates naturalness scores in LLM-judge evaluation. Use a different TTS engine for test utterance fixtures than for product output rendering.
